translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And in the first year of Cyrus king of Persia, that the word of Jehovah by the mouth of Jeremiah might be accomplished, Jehovah stirred up the spirit of Cyrus king of Persia, and he made a proclamation throughout his kingdom, and also in writing, saying,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus says Cyrus king of Persia: All the kingdoms of the earth has Jehovah theGod of the heavens given to me, and he has charged me to build him a house at Jerusalem, which is in Judah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whosoever there is among you of all his people, hisGod be with him, and let him go up to Jerusalem, which is in Judah, and build the house of Jehovah theGod of Israel — he isGod — which is at Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And whosoever remains in any place where he sojourns, let the men of his place help him with silver, and with gold, and with goods, and with beasts, besides the voluntary offering for the house ofGod which is at Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the chief fathers of Judah and Benjamin rose up, and the priests, and the Levites, even all those whose spiritGod had stirred, to go up to build the house of Jehovah which is at Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And all they that were about them strengthened their hands with articles of silver, with gold, with goods, and with beasts, and with precious things, besides all that was willingly offered.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And king Cyrus brought forth the vessels of the house of Jehovah, which Nebuchadnezzar had brought forth out of Jerusalem and had put in the house of hisgod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Cyrus king of Persia brought them forth by the hand of Mithredath the treasurer, who counted them out to Sheshbazzar the prince of Judah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this is the number of them: thirty chargers of gold, a thousand chargers of silver, nine-and-twenty knives,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: thirty basons of gold, silver basons of a second [sort] four hundred and ten, [and] other vessels a thousand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All the vessels of gold and of silver were five thousand four hundred. The whole did Sheshbazzar bring up, when they of the captivity were brought up from Babylon to Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these are the children of the province, that went up out of the captivity of those that had been carried away, whom Nebuchadnezzar the king of Babylon had carried away to Babylon, and who came again to Jerusalem and to Judah, every one to his city,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who came with Zerubbabel, Jeshua, Nehemiah, Saraiah, Reelaiah, Mordecai, Bilshan, Mispar, Bigvai, Rehum, Baanah. The number of the men of the people of Israel:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of Parosh, two thousand one hundred and seventy-two.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of Shephatiah, three hundred and seventy-two.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of Arah, seven hundred and seventy-five.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of Pahath-Moab, of the children of Jeshua [and] Joab, two thousand eight hundred and twelve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of Elam, a thousand two hundred and fifty-four.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of Zattu, nine hundred and forty-five.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of Zaccai, seven hundred and sixty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of Bani, six hundred and forty-two.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of Bebai, six hundred and twenty-three.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of Azgad, a thousand two hundred and twenty-two.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of Adonikam, six hundred and sixty-six.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of Bigvai, two thousand and fifty-six.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of Adin, four hundred and fifty-four.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of Ater of [the family of] Hezekiah, ninety-eight.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of Bezai, three hundred and twenty-three.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of Jorah, a hundred and twelve.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of Hashum, two hundred and twenty-three.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of Gibbar, ninety-five.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of Bethlehem, a hundred and twenty-three.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The men of Netophah, fifty-six.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The men of Anathoth, a hundred and twenty-eight.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of Azmaveth, forty-two.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of Kirjath-arim, Chephirah, and Beeroth, seven hundred and forty-three.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of Ramah and Geba, six hundred and twenty-one.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The men of Michmas, a hundred and twenty-two.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The men of Bethel and Ai, two hundred and twenty-three.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of Nebo, fifty-two.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of Magbish, a hundred and fifty-six.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of the other Elam, a thousand two hundred and fifty-four.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of Harim, three hundred and twenty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of Lod, Hadid, and Ono, seven hundred and twenty-five.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of Jericho, three hundred and forty-five.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of Senaah, three thousand six hundred and thirty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The priests: the children of Jedaiah, of the house of Jeshua, nine hundred and seventy-three.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of Immer, a thousand and fifty-two.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of Pashhur, a thousand two hundred and forty-seven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of Harim, a thousand and seventeen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Levites: the children of Jeshua and of Kadmiel, of the children of Hodaviah, seventy-four.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The singers: the children of Asaph, a hundred and twenty-eight.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of the doorkeepers: the children of Shallum, the children of Ater, the children of Talmon, the children of Akkub, the children of Hatita, the children of Shobai, in all a hundred and thirty-nine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Nethinim: the children of Ziha, the children of Hasupha, the children of Tabbaoth,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the children of Keros, the children of Siaha, the children of Padon,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the children of Lebanah, the children of Hagabah, the children of Akkub,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the children of Hagab, the children of Shamlai, the children of Hanan,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the children of Giddel, the children of Gahar, the children of Reaiah,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the children of Rezin, the children of Nekoda, the children of Gazzam,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the children of Uzza, the children of Phaseah, the children of Besai,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the children of Asnah, the children of Meunim, the children of Nephusim,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the children of Bakbuk, the children of Hakupha, the children of Harhur,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the children of Bazluth, the children of Mehida, the children of Harsha,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the children of Barkos, the children of Sisera, the children of Thamah,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the children of Neziah, the children of Hatipha.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of Solomon's servants: the children of Sotai, the children of Sophereth, the children of Peruda,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the children of Jaalah, the children of Darkon, the children of Giddel,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the children of Shephatiah, the children of Hattil, the children of Pochereth-Hazzebaim, the children of Ami.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All the Nethinim and the children of Solomon's servants, three hundred and ninety-two.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these are they that went up from Tel-melah, Tel-harsha, Cherub-Addan, Immer; but they could not shew their fathers' house, nor their seed, whether they were of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of Delaiah, the children of Tobijah, the children of Nekoda, six hundred and fifty-two.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And of the children of the priests: the children of Habaiah, the children of Koz, the children of Barzillai; who took a wife of the daughters of Barzillai the Gileadite, and was called after their name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: These sought their genealogical register, but they were not found; therefore were they, as polluted, removed from the priesthood.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Tirshatha said to them that they should not eat of the most holy things, till there stood up a priest with Urim and with Thummim.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The whole congregation together was forty-two thousand three hundred and sixty,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: besides their servants and their maids, of whom there were seven thousand three hundred and thirty-seven; and they had two hundred singing men and singing women.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Their horses were seven hundred and thirty-six; their mules two hundred and forty-five;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: their camels four hundred and thirty-five; the asses six thousand seven hundred and twenty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And some of the chief fathers, when they came to the house of Jehovah which is at Jerusalem, offered freely for the house ofGod to set it up in its place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They gave after their ability to the treasure of the work sixty-one thousand darics of gold, and five thousand pounds of silver, and one hundred priests' coats.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the priests, and the Levites, and [some] of the people, and the singers, and the doorkeepers, and the Nethinim dwelt in their cities, and all Israel in their cities.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the seventh month came, and the children of Israel were in the cities, the people gathered together as one man to Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then stood up Jeshua the son of Jozadak, and his brethren the priests, and Zerubbabel the son of Shealtiel, and his brethren, and built the altar of theGod of Israel, to offer up burnt-offerings on it, as it is written in the law of Moses the man ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they set the altar on its base; for fear was upon them because of the people of the countries; and they offered up burnt-offerings on it to Jehovah, the morning and evening burnt-offerings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they held the feast of tabernacles as it is written, and [offered] daily burnt-offerings by number, according to the ordinance, as the duty of every day required;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and afterwards the continual burnt-offering, and those of the new moons, and of all the set feasts of Jehovah that were consecrated, and of every one that willingly offered a voluntary offering to Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: From the first day of the seventh month they began to offer up burnt-offerings to Jehovah. But the foundation of the temple of Jehovah was not [yet] laid.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they gave money to the masons and to the carpenters; and meat and drink and oil to the Zidonians and to the Tyrians, to bring cedar-trees from Lebanon to the sea at Joppa, according to the grant that they had of Cyrus king of Persia.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And in the second year of their coming to the house ofGod at Jerusalem, in the second month, began Zerubbabel the son of Shealtiel, and Jeshua the son of Jozadak, and the remnant of their brethren the priests and the Levites, and all they that had come out of the captivity to Jerusalem; and they appointed the Levites, from twenty years old and upward, to superintend the work of the house of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Jeshua stood up, his sons and his brethren, Kadmiel and his sons, the sons of Judah, as one [man], to superintend the workmen in the house ofGod; [also] the sons of Henadad, their sons and their brethren, the Levites.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when the builders laid the foundation of the temple of Jehovah, they set the priests in their apparel, with trumpets, and the Levites the sons of Asaph, with cymbals, to praise Jehovah according to the directions of David king of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they sang alternately together in praising and giving thanks to Jehovah: For he is good, for his loving-kindness [endureth] for ever toward Israel. And all the people shouted with a great shout to the praise of Jehovah, because the foundation of the house of Jehovah was laid.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But many of the priests and Levites and chief fathers, the ancient men that had seen the first house, wept with a loud voice, [when] the foundation of this house was laid in their sight; and many shouted aloud for joy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the people could not discern the noise of the shout of joy from the noise of the weeping of the people; for the people shouted with a great shout, and the noise was heard afar off.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the adversaries of Judah and Benjamin heard that the children of the captivity were building the temple to Jehovah theGod of Israel;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they came to Zerubbabel and to the chief fathers, and said to them, We would build with you; for we seek yourGod, as ye; and we have sacrificed to him since the days of Esar-haddon king of Assyria, who brought us up hither.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Zerubbabel and Jeshua and the rest of the chief fathers of Israel said to them, Ye have nothing to do with us to build a house to ourGod, but we alone will build to Jehovah theGod of Israel, as king Cyrus, the king of Persia, has commanded us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the people of the land weakened the hands of the people of Judah, and troubled them in building;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they hired counsellors against them, to frustrate their purpose, all the days of Cyrus king of Persia, even until the reign of Darius king of Persia.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And in the reign of Ahasuerus, in the beginning of his reign, they wrote an accusation against the inhabitants of Judah and Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And in the days of Artaxerxes, Bishlam, Mithredath, Tabeel, and the rest of his companions, wrote to Artaxerxes king of Persia; and the writing of the letter was written in Aramaic, and interpreted in Aramaic.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Rehum the chancellor and Shimshai the scribe wrote a letter against Jerusalem to Artaxerxes the king after this sort:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Rehum the chancellor, and Shimshai the scribe, and the rest of their companions, the Dinaites, and the Apharsathchites, the Tarpelites, the Apharsites, the Archevites, the Babylonians, the Shushanchites, the Dehaites, the Elamites,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and the rest of the peoples whom the great and noble Osnappar brought over and settled in the cities of Samaria, and the rest [of the country] on this side the river, and so forth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This is the copy of the letter that they sent to him: To Artaxerxes the king: Thy servants the men on this side the river, and so forth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be it known to the king that the Jews who came up from thee unto us have come to Jerusalem; they are building the rebellious and the bad city, and they complete the walls and join up the foundations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be it known therefore unto the king, that, if this city be built and the walls be completed, they will not pay tribute, tax, and toll, and in the end it will bring damage to the kings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now, since we eat the salt of the palace, and it is not right for us to see the king's injury, therefore have we sent and informed the king;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that search may be made in the book of the annals of thy fathers: so shalt thou find in the book of the annals and know that this city is a rebellious city, which has done damage to kings and provinces, and that they have raised sedition within the same of old time, for which cause this city was destroyed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: We inform the king that if this city be built and its walls be completed, by this means thou shalt have no portion on this side the river.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The king sent an answer to Rehum the chancellor, and Shimshai the scribe, and the rest of their companions that dwell in Samaria, and the other places beyond the river: Peace, and so forth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The letter that ye sent to us has been read before me distinctly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I gave orders, and search has been made, and it has been found that this city of old time has made insurrection against the kings, and that rebellion and sedition have been raised therein.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there have been mighty kings over Jerusalem, who have ruled over all beyond the river; and tribute, tax, and toll were paid to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now give order to make these men to cease, and that this city be not built, until the order shall be given from me;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and take heed that ye fail not to do this: why should harm grow to the damage of the kings?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: As soon as the copy of king Artaxerxes' letter was read before Rehum, and Shimshai the scribe, and their companions, they went up in haste to Jerusalem to the Jews, and made them cease by force and power.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then ceased the work of the house ofGod which is at Jerusalem; and it ceased until the second year of the reign of Darius king of Persia.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now the prophets, Haggai the prophet and Zechariah the son of Iddo, prophesied to the Jews that were in Judah and Jerusalem; in the name of theGod of Israel [did they prophesy] to them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then rose up Zerubbabel the son of Shealtiel, and Jeshua the son of Jozadak, and began to build the house ofGod which is at Jerusalem; and with them were the prophets ofGod, who helped them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: At that time came to them Tatnai, governor on this side the river, and Shethar-boznai, and their companions, and said thus to them: Who gave you orders to build this house and to complete this wall?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they said to them after this manner: What are the names of the men that build this building?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the eye of theirGod was upon the elders of the Jews, and they did not make them cease till the matter came to Darius; and then they returned answer by letter concerning it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The copy of the letter that Tatnai, governor on this side the river, and Shethar-boznai, and his companions the Apharsachites, who were on this side the river, sent to Darius the king.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They sent a report to him in which was written thus: To Darius the king, all peace!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be it known to the king that we went into the province of Judah, to the house of the greatGod, which is being built with great stones, and timber is laid in the walls, and this work is being carried on with diligence, and prospers in their hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then asked we those elders: thus we said to them, Who gave you orders to build this house and to complete this wall?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: We asked their names also, to inform thee, that we might write the names of the men that were the chief of them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thus they returned us answer, saying, We are the servants of theGod of the heavens and the earth, and build the house that was built these many years ago; and a great king of Israel built and completed it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But after that our fathers had provoked theGod of the heavens to wrath, he gave them into the hand of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon, the Chaldean, and he destroyed this house, and carried the people away unto Babylon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But in the first year of Cyrus king of Babylon, king Cyrus gave orders to build this house ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the vessels also of gold and silver of the house ofGod, which Nebuchadnezzar took out of the temple that was at Jerusalem and brought into the temple of Babylon, those did king Cyrus take out of the temple of Babylon, and they were delivered to one Sheshbazzar by name, whom he had appointed governor.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he said to him, Take these vessels, go, carry them into the temple that is at Jerusalem, and let the house ofGod be built in its place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then came the same Sheshbazzar, [and] laid the foundation of the house ofGod which is at Jerusalem; and since that time even until now has it been in building, and it is not completed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, if it seem good to the king, let search be made in the king's treasure-house, which is there at Babylon, whether it be so, that orders were given by king Cyrus to build this house ofGod at Jerusalem; and let the king send his pleasure to us concerning this matter.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then king Darius gave orders, and search was made in the house of the rolls, where the treasures were laid up in Babylon.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there was found at Achmetha in the fortress that is in the province of Media a roll, and therein was a record thus written:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the first year of king Cyrus, king Cyrus made a decree [concerning] the house ofGod at Jerusalem: Let the house be built for a place where they offer sacrifices, and let its foundations be solidly laid; its height sixty cubits, its breadth sixty cubits,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [with] three rows of great stones, and a row of new timber; and let the expenses be given out of the king's house:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and also let the golden and silver vessels of the house ofGod, which Nebuchadnezzar took out of the temple that is at Jerusalem and brought to Babylon, be restored and brought again to the temple that is at Jerusalem, in their place; and thou shalt put [them] in the house ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore Tatnai, governor beyond the river, Shethar-boznai, and your companions the Apharsachites, who are beyond the river, be ye far from thence:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: let the work of this house ofGod alone; let the governor of the Jews and the elders of the Jews build this house ofGod in its place.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Moreover, I give orders what ye shall do to these elders of the Jews, for the building of this house ofGod: that of the king's goods, of the tribute beyond the river, expenses be diligently given to these men, that they be not hindered.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And that which they have need of, both young bullocks and rams and lambs, for the burnt-offerings to theGod of the heavens, wheat, salt, wine, and oil, according to the appointment of the priests that are at Jerusalem, let it be given them day by day without fail;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that they may present sweet odours to theGod of the heavens, and pray for the life of the king and of his sons.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Also I have given order that whosoever shall alter this rescript, let timber be pulled down from his house, and being set up, let him be hanged thereon, and let his house be made a dunghill for this.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And theGod that has caused his name to dwell there overthrow every king and people that shall put forth their hand to alter [or] to destroy this house ofGod which is at Jerusalem. I Darius have given [this] order; let it be done diligently.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Tatnai, governor on this side the river, Shethar-boznai, and their companions, because of that which king Darius had sent, did so diligently.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the elders of the Jews built; and they prospered through the prophesying of Haggai the prophet and Zechariah the son of Iddo. And they built and completed [it] according to the commandment of theGod of Israel, and according to the commandment of Cyrus, and Darius, and Artaxerxes king of Persia.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this house was finished on the third day of the month Adar, which was in the sixth year of the reign of king Darius.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel, the priests and the Levites, and the rest of the children of the captivity, kept the dedication of this house ofGod with joy;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they presented at the dedication of this house ofGod a hundred bullocks, two hundred rams, four hundred lambs, and for a sin-offering for all Israel, twelve he-goats, according to the number of the tribes of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they set the priests in their classes, and the Levites in their divisions, for the service ofGod, which is at Jerusalem: as it is written in the book of Moses.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of the captivity held the passover upon the fourteenth of the first month.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the priests and the Levites had purified themselves as one [man]: they were all pure; and they killed the passover for all the children of the captivity, and for their brethren the priests, and for themselves.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Israel that were come back out of captivity, and all such as had separated themselves to them from the filthiness of the nations of the land, to seek Jehovah theGod of Israel, did eat;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and they kept the feast of unleavened bread seven days with joy; for Jehovah had made them joyful, and turned the heart of the king of Assyria to them, to strengthen their hands in the work of the house ofGod, theGod of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And after these things, in the reign of Artaxerxes, king of Persia, Ezra the son of Seraiah, the son of Azariah, the son of Hilkijah,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the son of Shallum, the son of Zadok, the son of Ahitub,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the son of Amariah, the son of Azariah, the son of Meraioth,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the son of Zerahiah, the son of Uzzi, the son of Bukki,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the son of Abishua, the son of Phinehas, the son of Eleazar, the son of Aaron the chief priest,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — this Ezra went up from Babylon; and he was a ready scribe in the law of Moses, which Jehovah theGod of Israel had given. And the king granted him all his request, according to the hand of Jehovah hisGod upon him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (And there went up [some] of the children of Israel, and of the priests, and the Levites, and the singers, and the doorkeepers, and the Nethinim, to Jerusalem, in the seventh year of Artaxerxes the king.)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he came to Jerusalem in the fifth month, which was in the seventh year of the king.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For upon the first of the first month the project of going up from Babylon was determined on, and on the first of the fifth month he came to Jerusalem, according to the good hand of hisGod upon him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For Ezra had directed his heart to seek the law of Jehovah and to do it, and to teach in Israel the statutes and the ordinances.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And this is the copy of the letter that king Artaxerxes gave to Ezra the priest, the scribe, a scribe of the words of the commandments of Jehovah, and of his statutes to Israel:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Artaxerxes, king of kings, to Ezra the priest, an accomplished scribe of the law of theGod of the heavens, and so forth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I have given orders that all they of the people of Israel, and of their priests and the Levites, in my realm, who are disposed to go to Jerusalem, go with thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Because thou art sent by the king, and by his seven counsellors, to inquire concerning Judah and Jerusalem, according to the law of thyGod which is in thy hand;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and to carry the silver and gold which the king and his counsellors have freely offered to theGod of Israel, whose habitation is at Jerusalem,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and all the silver and gold that thou canst find in all the province of Babylon, besides the voluntary offering of the people, and of the priests, who offer willingly for the house of theirGod which is at Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Therefore thou shalt buy diligently with this money bullocks, rams, lambs, with their oblations and their drink-offerings, and offer them upon the altar of the house of yourGod which is at Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And whatever shall seem good to thee and to thy brethren to do with the rest of the silver and the gold, that do according to the will of yourGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the vessels that have been given thee for the service of the house of thyGod, deliver before theGod of Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And whatever more shall be needful for the house of thyGod which thou shalt have occasion to bestow, bestow it out of the king's treasure house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I, I Artaxerxes the king, do give orders to all the treasurers that are beyond the river, that whatever Ezra the priest and scribe of the law of theGod of the heavens shall require of you, it be done diligently,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: unto a hundred talents of silver, and to a hundred measures of wheat, and to a hundred baths of wine, and to a hundred baths of oil, and salt without prescribing [how much].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whatever is commanded by theGod of the heavens, let it be carefully done for the house of theGod of the heavens; for why should there be wrath against the realm of the king and his sons?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Also we inform you, as regards all the priests and Levites, singers, doorkeepers, Nethinim, and ministers of this house ofGod, it shall not be lawful to impose tribute, tax, and toll upon them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou, Ezra, after the wisdom of thyGod, which is in thy hand, set magistrates and judges who may judge all the people that are beyond the river, all such as know the laws of thyGod; and teach ye him that knows [them] not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And whosoever will not do the law of thyGod and the law of the king, let judgment be executed diligently upon him, whether unto death, or to banishment, or to confiscation of goods, or to imprisonment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Blessed be Jehovah theGod of our fathers, who has put [such a thing] as this in the king's heart, to beautify the house of Jehovah which is at Jerusalem;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and has extended mercy to me before the king and his counsellors, and before all the king's mighty princes. And I was strengthened, as the hand of Jehovah myGod was upon me; and I gathered together out of Israel chief men to go up with me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these are the chief fathers, and their genealogy, who went up with me from Babylon in the reign of Artaxerxes the king.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of the children of Phinehas, Gershom; of the children of Ithamar, Daniel; of the children of David, Hattush;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: of the children of Shecaniah, of the children of Parosh, Zechariah, and with him were registered by genealogy of the males a hundred and fifty.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of the children of Pahath-Moab, Elihoenai the son of Zerahiah, and with him two hundred males.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of the children of Shechaniah, the son of Jahaziel, and with him three hundred males.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And of the children of Adin, Ebed the son of Jonathan, and with him fifty males.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And of the children of Elam, Isaiah the son of Athaliah, and with him seventy males.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And of the children of Shephatiah, Zebadiah the son of Michael, and with him eighty males.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of the children of Joab, Obadiah the son of Jehiel, and with him two hundred and eighteen males.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And of the children of Shelomith, the son of Josiphiah, and with him a hundred and sixty males.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And of the children of Bebai, Zechariah the son of Bebai, and with him twenty-eight males.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And of the children of Azgad, Johanan the son of Hakkatan, and with him a hundred and ten males.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And of the children of Adonikam, the last ones, whose names are these: Eliphelet, Jeiel, and Shemaiah, and with them sixty males.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And of the children of Bigvai, Uthai and Zabbud, and with them seventy males.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I gathered them together at the river that runs to Ahava; and there we encamped three days; and I surveyed the people and the priests, and found none of the sons of Levi there.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I sent for Eliezer, for Ariel, for Shemaiah, and for Elnathan, and for Jarib, and for Elnathan, and for Nathan, and for Zechariah, and for Meshullam, chief men, and for Joiarib and for Elnathan, men of understanding.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I gave them a commission to Iddo the chief, at the place Casiphia, and I put words in their mouths to say to Iddo [and] his brethren the Nethinim, at the place Casiphia, that they should bring to us ministers for the house of ourGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And by the good hand of ourGod upon us, they brought us a man of understanding, of the sons of Mahli, the son of Levi, the son of Israel, namely, Sherebiah, with his sons and his brethren, eighteen;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Hashabiah, and with him Isaiah of the sons of Merari, his brethren and their sons, twenty;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and of the Nethinim, whom David and the princes had appointed for the service of the Levites, two hundred and twenty Nethinim: all of them were expressed by name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I proclaimed a fast there, at the river Ahava, that we might humble ourselves before ourGod, to seek of him a right way for us, and for our little ones, and for all our substance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For I was ashamed to require of the king a band of soldiers and horsemen to help us against the enemy in the way; for we had spoken to the king saying, The hand of ourGod is upon all them for good that seek him; but his power and his anger is against all them that forsake him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And we fasted, and besought ourGod for this; and he was entreated of us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I separated twelve of the chiefs of the priests, Sherebiah, Hashabiah, and ten of their brethren with them,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and I weighed to them the silver and the gold and the vessels, the heave-offering for the house of ourGod, which the king and his counsellors and his princes, and all Israel present, had offered.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I weighed into their hand six hundred and fifty talents of silver; and silver vessels a hundred talents, [and] of gold a hundred talents;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and twenty basons of gold, of a thousand darics; and two vessels of shining copper, precious as gold.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I said to them, Ye are holy unto Jehovah; the vessels also are holy; and the silver and the gold is a voluntary offering to Jehovah theGod of your fathers.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Watch and keep [them] until ye weigh them before the chiefs of the priests and the Levites, and the chiefs of the fathers of Israel, at Jerusalem, in the chambers of the house of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the priests and the Levites received by weight the silver and the gold and the vessels, to bring them to Jerusalem unto the house of ourGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And we departed from the river Ahava on the twelfth of the first month, to go to Jerusalem; and the hand of ourGod was upon us, and he delivered us from the hand of the enemy, and of such as lay in wait by the way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And we came to Jerusalem, and abode there three days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And on the fourth day the silver and the gold and the vessels were weighed in the house of ourGod into the hand of Meremoth the son of Urijah the priest; and with him was Eleazar the son of Phinehas; and with them were Jozabad the son of Jeshua, and Noadiah the son of Binnui, Levites:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the whole by number and by weight; and all the weight was written down at that time.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The children of those that had been carried away, who had come out of the captivity, presented burnt-offerings to theGod of Israel, twelve bullocks for all Israel, ninety-six rams, seventy-seven lambs, twelve he-goats for a sin-offering: all for a burnt-offering to Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they delivered the king's edicts to the king's satraps, and to the governors on this side the river. And they furthered the people and the house ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now when these things were completed, the princes came to me, saying, The people of Israel, and the priests and the Levites, have not separated themselves from the peoples of the lands, according to their abominations, [even] of the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Jebusites, the Ammonites, the Moabites, the Egyptians, and the Amorites;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for they have taken of their daughters for themselves and for their sons, and have mingled the holy seed with the peoples of the lands; and the hand of the princes and rulers has been chief in this unfaithfulness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And when I heard this thing, I rent my mantle and my garment, and plucked off the hair of my head and of my beard, and sat down overwhelmed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then were assembled to me every one that trembled at the words of theGod of Israel, because of the unfaithfulness of those that had been carried away; and I sat overwhelmed until the evening oblation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And at the evening oblation I arose up from my humiliation; and with my mantle and my garment rent, I fell on my knees, and spread out my hands to Jehovah myGod,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and said: O myGod, I am ashamed and blush to lift up my face to thee, myGod; for our iniquities are increased over [our] head, and our trespass is grown up to the heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Since the days of our fathers, we have been in great trespass to this day; and for our iniquities we, our kings, our priests, have been given into the hand of the kings of the lands, to the sword, and to captivity, and to spoil, and to confusion of face, as it is this day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now for a little space there hath been favour from Jehovah ourGod, to leave us a remnant to escape, and to give us a nail in his holy place, that ourGod may lighten our eyes, and give us a little reviving in our bondage.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For we are bondmen; yet ourGod hath not forsaken us in our bondage, but hath extended mercy unto us before the kings of Persia, to give us a reviving, to set up the house of ourGod and to repair the ruins thereof, and to give us a wall in Judah and in Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now, what shall we say, ourGod, after this? for we have forsaken thy commandments,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: which thou hast commanded by thy servants the prophets, saying, The land, unto which ye go to possess [it], is an unclean land through the filthiness of the peoples of the lands, through their abominations with which they have filled it from one end to another through their uncleanness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now therefore give not your daughters to their sons, neither take their daughters to your sons, nor seek their peace or their prosperity for ever; that ye may be strong, and eat the good of the land, and leave it for an inheritance to your children for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And after all that is come upon us for our evil deeds and for our great trespass, seeing that thou ourGod hast punished us less than our iniquities [deserve], and hast given us such deliverance as this,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: should we again break thy commandments, and join in affinity with the peoples of these abominations? wouldest thou not be angry with us till thou hadst consumed us, so that there should be no remnant nor any to escape?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah,God of Israel, thou art righteous; for we are a remnant that is escaped, as [it is] this day. Behold, we are before thee in our trespasses; for there is no standing before thee because of this.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And while Ezra prayed, and made confession, weeping and falling down before the house ofGod, there were gathered to him out of Israel a very great congregation of men and women and children; for the people wept very much.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Shechaniah the son of Jehiel, of the sons of Elam, answered and said to Ezra, We have acted unfaithfully toward ourGod, and have taken foreign wives of the peoples of the land; yet now there is hope for Israel concerning this thing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now let us make a covenant with ourGod to put away all the wives, and such as are born of them, according to the counsel of [my] lord, and of those that tremble at the commandments of ourGod; and let it be done according to the law.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Arise, for this matter is incumbent on thee, and we will be with thee: be of good courage, and do [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then Ezra arose, and made the chiefs of the priests, of the Levites, and of all Israel, to swear that they would do according to this word. And they swore.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ezra arose from before the house ofGod, and went into the chamber of Jehohanan the son of Eliashib; and when he came thither, he ate no bread and drank no water; for he mourned because of the unfaithfulness of them that had been carried away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they made proclamation in Judah and Jerusalem to all the children of the captivity, that they should gather themselves together unto Jerusalem;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and that whosoever would not come within three days, according to the counsel of the princes and the elders, all his substance should be confiscated, and himself separated from the congregation of those that had been carried away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then were all the men of Judah and Benjamin gathered together at Jerusalem within three days. It was the ninth month, on the twentieth of the month; and all the people sat in the open space of the house ofGod, trembling because of the matter, and because of the pouring rain.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Ezra the priest stood up and said to them, Ye have acted unfaithfully, and have taken foreign wives, to increase the trespass of Israel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And now make confession to Jehovah theGod of your fathers, and do his pleasure, and separate yourselves from the peoples of the land, and from the foreign wives.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the whole congregation answered and said with a loud voice, Yes, it is for us to do according to thy words.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the people are many, and it is a time of pouring rain, and it is not possible to stand without: neither is this a work for one day or two; for we are many that have transgressed in this thing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let now our princes, while this matter is going on, stand for all the congregation, and let all those that have taken foreign wives in our cities come at the appointed times, and with them the elders of every city, and the judges thereof, until the fierce anger of ourGod be turned from us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Only Jonathan the son of Asahel and Jahzeiah the son of Tikvah stood up against this; and Meshullam and Shabbethai the Levite helped them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of the captivity did so. And Ezra the priest [and] certain of the chief fathers were separated according to their fathers' houses, and all of them [expressed] by name; and they sat down on the first day of the tenth month to examine the matter.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they ended with all the men that had taken foreign wives by the first day of the first month.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And among the sons of the priests there were found that had taken foreign wives, of the sons of Jeshua the son of Jozadak, and his brethren: Maaseiah, and Eliezer, and Jarib, and Gedaliah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they gave their hand to send away their wives; and they offered a ram of the flock, as trespass-offering for their guilt.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And of the children of Immer: Hanani, and Zebadiah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And of the children of Harim: Maaseiah, and Elijah, and Shemaiah, and Jehiel, and Uzziah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And of the children of Pashhur: Elioenai, Maaseiah, Ishmael, Nethaneel, Jozabad, and Elasah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And of the Levites: Jozabad, and Shimei, and Kelaiah (that is, Kelita), Pethahiah, Judah, and Eliezer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And of the singers: Eliashib. And of the doorkeepers: Shallum, and Telem, and Uri.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And of Israel; of the children of Parosh: Ramiah, and Jizzijah, and Malchijah, and Mijamin, and Eleazar, and Malchijah, and Benaiah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And of the children of Elam: Mattaniah, Zechariah, and Jehiel, and Abdi, and Jeremoth, and Elijah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And of the children of Zattu: Elioenai, Eliashib, Mattaniah, and Jeremoth, and Zabad, and Aziza.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And of the children of Bebai: Jehohanan, Hananiah, Zabbai, Athlai.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And of the children of Bani: Meshullam, Malluch, and Adaiah, Jashub, and Sheal, and Ramoth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And of the children of Pahath-Moab: Adna, and Chelal, Benaiah, Maaseiah, Mattaniah, Bezaleel, and Binnui, and Manasseh.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the children of Harim: Eliezer, Jishijah, Malchijah, Shemaiah, Simeon,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Benjamin, Malluch, Shemariah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of the children of Hashum: Mattenai, Mattattah, Zabad, Eliphelet, Jeremai, Manasseh, Shimei.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of the sons of Bani: Maadai, Amram, and Uel,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Benaiah, Bediah, Cheluhu,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Vaniah, Meremoth, Eliashib,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Mattaniah, Mattenai, and Jaasai,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Bani, and Binnui, Shimei,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and Shelemiah, and Nathan, and Adaiah,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Machnadbai, Shashai, Sharai,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Azareel, and Shelemiah, Shemariah,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Shallum, Amariah, Joseph.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Of the children of Nebo: Jeiel, Mattithiah, Zabad, Zebina, Jaddai, and Joel, Benaiah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All these had taken foreign wives; and there were among them wives who had had children.
